div.head: The Minister-Counselor of Embassy in China (Clark) to the Secretary of State

opener.dateline:
Canton, June 3, 1949—9 p. m.
[Received June 4—12:38 p. m.]

div.p: Cantel 529. Legislative Yuan approved Yen Hsi-shan as premier this morning by 254 to 56 with 10 ballots invalid. Entire process took only little over 1 hour including half-hour insipid pro-Yen speeches. Few scattered handclaps when results announced.

Easy majority favoring Yen probably accounted for by: (1) C–C Clique satisfied Yen definitely not hostile toward Gimo;Generalissimo Chiang Kai-Shek, who retired as President of the Republic of China on January 21, 1949. (2) Legislative
Yuan anxiety recess for summer and board ships now awaiting take them Formosa. Interesting to note that Yen flew Formosa yesterday ostensibly to bury mother.
Sent Department Cantel 529; repeated Nanking 360; Shanghai 307.
